title: Execute an R script or inline R code
summary: Execute an R script file or inline R code. Stdout is captured line-by-line as Progress events and written to run.log. Stderr is captured line-by-line as Warning events and written to stderr.log. A non-zero exit code produces Finished { success: false }.
description:
Runs an R script (via `Rscript` or `R --no-save --no-restore --file`) and streams its output back to the Open Choice protocol. Either provide a path to an `.R` file via `script`, or supply inline code via `code` — the two fields are mutually exclusive. Positional and named arguments can be forwarded to the script via `args` and `kwargs`. All output files (run.log, stderr.log) are written to `output_dir`.

The `trust_acknowledged` field must be set to `true`. This is an explicit safety gate: the plugin executes arbitrary R code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process. Only run scripts from sources you trust.
usage:
[["r-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "<path/to/script.R>"
output_dir = "<dir>"
trust_acknowledged = true
# optional:
args = ["arg1", "arg2"]
kwargs = { key = "value" }
executable = "Rscript"

arguments.name: script
arguments.description: Path to the .R file to execute. Mutually exclusive with code. Relative paths are resolved from the directory containing the .oce file. Absolute paths are used as-is.

arguments.name: code
arguments.description: Inline R source code to run. Mutually exclusive with script. Written to a temporary .R file and executed. Use TOML multiline strings ("""...""" or '''...''') for multi-line code.

arguments.name: args
arguments.description: Positional arguments appended after the script path. Accessed in R via commandArgs(trailingOnly=TRUE).

arguments.name: kwargs
arguments.description: Named arguments passed as --key value pairs after positional args. Accessed in R via commandArgs(trailingOnly=TRUE) or an arg-parser library.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory where run.log and stderr.log are written. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: executable
arguments.description: R executable to use. Defaults to "Rscript". Use "R" to invoke R --no-save --no-restore --file instead.

arguments.name: trust_acknowledged
arguments.description: Must be true. Explicitly acknowledges that this plugin executes arbitrary R code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process, and that the script source is trusted.

details:
`run_script` is a thin execution wrapper: it invokes `Rscript` (or `R`) on the provided script or inline code, streams stdout line-by-line as Progress events, and writes all output to `output_dir/run.log`. Stderr lines are captured as Warning events and written to `output_dir/stderr.log`.

The `trust_acknowledged = true` field is required and intentional — it prevents accidental execution of untrusted code by requiring the caller to explicitly opt in.

Argument forwarding:
- `args` becomes positional arguments after the script path on the command line
- `kwargs` becomes `--key value` pairs following the positional args
In R, retrieve them with `commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)`. For structured parsing, use a library like `optparse` or `argparse`.

For inline code use TOML multiline strings to keep the envelope readable.
value:
On success, `output_dir` contains:

- `run.log` — all stdout lines from the R process.
- `stderr.log` — all stderr lines from the R process.

The plugin emits `event.run.started`, per-line Progress events, and `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed` on non-zero exit).
notes: Exactly one of `script` or `code` must be provided — supplying both or neither is an error.
The `trust_acknowledged` field must be `true`; any other value (including omitting it) causes the command to reject immediately.
Non-zero exit codes from R produce `event.run.failed`; the log files are still written.
Use `executable = "R"` if your script relies on interactive R session semantics or uses `.Rprofile`.

see_also.description: Python equivalent — run a .py file or pass a virtualenv interpreter.
Julia equivalent — run a .jl file and optionally activate a Julia project environment.

examples.title: Run an R script with default Rscript
Pass kwargs to the script (e.g. input/output paths)
examples.body:
[["r-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/export_partworths.R"
output_dir = "./runs/r-export"
kwargs = { input = "./runs/hb-stan/part_worths.csv", output = "./outputs/partworths_clean.csv" }
trust_acknowledged = true

examples.title: Run inline R code (quick sanity check)
examples.body:
[["r-wrapper::run_script"]]
code = """
cat('R version:', R.version$major, R.version$minor, '\n')
cat('Working directory:', getwd(), '\n')
"""
output_dir = "./runs/r-check"
trust_acknowledged = true

examples.title: Run a script with positional args using the R executable
examples.body:
[["r-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/batch_model.R"
args = ["fold_01", "fold_02", "fold_03"]
output_dir = "./runs/r-batch"
executable = "R"
trust_acknowledged = true

fields.name: script
fields.description: Path to .R file. Mutually exclusive with code.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Inline R code. Mutually exclusive with script.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Positional args forwarded to the script via commandArgs(trailingOnly).

fields.name: kwargs
fields.description: Named args forwarded as --key value pairs.

fields.name: trust_acknowledged
fields.description: Must be true. Safety gate for arbitrary code execution.

artifacts.title: Script stdout log
artifacts.description: Every line written to stdout by the script, captured in order. Review this to see printed results, progress messages, and any output the script produced.

artifacts.title: Script stderr log
artifacts.description: Every line written to stderr by the script, captured as warnings. Review this first if the run fails — error messages and stack traces appear here.
